description:
A routine integrity-audit has been scheduled for this machine.  
    You are acting as the backup engineer and must record the exact versions of a few core utilities as well as the checksum of the system’s APT source list.

    1. Create a directory named “audit” inside your home folder:  /home/user/audit  
       (create it only if it is missing).

    2. Inside that directory create a **single** text file whose name is  
          pkg_audit_YYYYMMDD.log  
       where *YYYYMMDD* is today’s calendar date in the machine’s local time zone (e.g. 20240815).

    3. The file **must contain three clearly-delimited sections, in the order shown below, each section separated from the next by exactly one blank line (i.e. two consecutive newline characters).**  
       Every line in the file, including the last one, must end with a single newline character “\n”.

       Section layout & required content:

       ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
       [PACKAGE VERSIONS]  
       coreutils:<version_string>  
       tar:<version_string>  
       gzip:<version_string>

       [APT SOURCES CHECK]  
       <sha256_of_/etc/apt/sources.list>

       [SUMMARY]  
       Audit completed successfully on YYYY-MM-DD at HH:MM
       ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

       Details of each field:

       • <version_string> is exactly what is returned by  
         dpkg-query -W -f='${Version}' <package_name>  

       • <sha256_of_/etc/apt/sources.list> is the 64-character hexadecimal SHA-256 digest
         of the file /etc/apt/sources.list (output should contain ONLY the hash, no spaces,
         filenames, or extra text).

       • The date/time in the SUMMARY sentence must match the local date/time
         at the moment the log is generated, formatted as ISO date “YYYY-MM-DD”
         and 24-hour time “HH:MM”.

    4. Double-check that:
         • There are exactly 3 section headers and they appear verbatim as given above.  
         • There is exactly one blank line between sections.  
         • No additional characters, spaces, or commentary appear anywhere else in the log.

    The automated grader will read /home/user/audit/pkg_audit_*.log and will fail
    if any of the following are true:
       – The file is missing or named incorrectly.  
       – The three sections are not found in the right order.  
       – A package version string is empty or the package name is misspelled.  
       – The SHA-256 digest does not match the digest of /etc/apt/sources.list.  
       – The SUMMARY line is not formatted exactly as required.

    Hints (do NOT show commands):
        • dpkg-query is the preferred way to extract package versions.  
        • sha256sum prints both the hash and the filename; you may have to trim the filename.  
        • Use the date command for time stamping.  

    This entire task should take roughly 5–10 terminal commands when executed efficiently.
truth:
<!-- Paths and filename pattern the grader will look for -->
    expected_directory: /home/user/audit
    expected_filename_pattern: pkg_audit_????????.log   <!-- eight digits = YYYYMMDD -->

    <!-- Section headers that must be found exactly -->
    section1_header: [PACKAGE VERSIONS]
    section2_header: [APT SOURCES CHECK]
    section3_header: [SUMMARY]

    <!-- Package names whose versions must be captured -->
    required_packages:
      - coreutils
      - tar
      - gzip

    <!-- File whose sha256 must appear in section 2 -->
    checksum_target: /etc/apt/sources.list

    <!-- FORMAT VALIDATION DETAILS (used by the grader) -->
    section_delimiter_regex: ^\s*$     <!-- Exactly one completely blank line between sections -->
    package_line_regex: ^(coreutils|tar|gzip):[0-9A-Za-z\.\+\:\~\-]+$
    sha256_regex: ^[A-Fa-f0-9]{64}$
    summary_line_regex: ^Audit completed successfully on \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} at \d{2}:\d{2}$

    <!-- The grader will dynamically compute these, but placeholders are included for clarity -->
    dynamic_package_versions:
        coreutils: <auto-detect>
        tar: <auto-detect>
        gzip: <auto-detect>
    dynamic_sha256: <auto-computed>

    <!-- End of privileged verification data -->
name: task_000000_dbdccf81
